name: Dark Matter Battery
tips: Dark Matter Battery deals damage to the opponent's 🤍 equal to its own Dark Matter stacks in your Decay Phase. At high DM, this is a win condition by itself.
At range 2, Battery can sit in the back row and ping the opponent's life total without ever entering combat. Protect it with front-line minions.
Unlike other DM cards, Battery is a Machine, not a Mage — it doesn't benefit from Mage-specific DM buffs. Its value is purely in the Decay Phase damage.

Battery can't gain DM from Mage-only sources like Shady Trade Deal and Dark Matter Stash — it's tribe Machine, not Mage. Grave Caller's activated ability gives it +🗡️ equal to Grave Caller's DM (useful to turn Battery into an attacker), but does not grant it Dark Matter stacks.
At 0🗡️/20🤍, Battery can't attack normally. Its entire value is the Decay Phase damage. If the opponent ignores it, the damage adds up fast; if they focus it, your other minions operate freely.
rulings: Damage fires once per full turn cycle, in the owner's Decay Phase (the end of the owner's turn). scale_with=dark_matter uses the Battery's own DM stacks — not a pooled player total.
At Battery DM=0, the damage is 0 and the effect is skipped entirely (no visible tick).

No current source grants Battery DM: Mage-tribe-only sources (Dark Matter Stash, Shady Trade Deal) skip it, and Grave Caller's activated ability grants bonus 🗡️ scaled by Grave Caller's DM — it does not grant Dark Matter stacks.
If the opponent's HP reaches 0 from this damage, they lose immediately — the effect checks win condition during passive resolution.
